title: No milestones block — add a milestone block kind (samples catalog + active .context)
description: There is no milestone block kind, yet the phase and story schemas already name 'milestone' as a cross-altitude edge target. Add a milestone block. Fields: id (MILE-NNN), name, binary outcome-based success_criteria, status (planned | reached — NO 'missed', which is useless), and an OPTIONAL release field so various gaps / issues / tasks / phases can be rolled into a release (e.g. 'and this is 0.30.1'). No intent field — instead a milestone has edges to stories. Relationships are closure-table edges, not inline. The milestone block must exist in BOTH the packaged samples catalog AND the active .context substrate here; pi-context update (the CLI) adds it into samples.

evidence.reference: block_kinds registry lists 17 kinds (decisions, framework-gaps, tasks, verification, issues, features, research, rationale, spec-reviews, layer-plans, requirements, conventions, context-contracts, phase, story, work-orders, session-notes) — none is 'milestone'
phase description references story -> milestone/phase/project-goal edges, but no milestone block kind exists
story description names milestone as a cross-altitude edge target; no milestone block backs it

impact: Phases and stories reference milestones as edge targets with nothing to point at, and there is no block to roll gaps/issues/tasks/phases into a release — milestones cannot be represented in the substrate.
proposed_resolution: Add a milestone block kind with fields id (MILE-NNN), name, binary outcome-based success_criteria, status (planned | reached, no missed), and optional release; no intent field, edges to stories instead; relationships as closure-table edges. Land it in BOTH the packaged samples catalog (added via the pi-context update CLI) AND the active .context substrate.
